status.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText:
[Congressional Record Volume 171, Number 74 (Monday, May 5, 2025)]
[House]
From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]
By Mr. BEGICH:
H.R. 3176.
Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuant
to the following:
Congress has the authority to enact this legislation
pursuant to Article I, Section 8, Clause 3 of the United
States Constitution, which grants Congress the power to
regulate commerce with foreign nations, among the several
states, and with the Indian tribes. The National Volcano
Early Warning and Monitoring System supports the monitoring
and early warning of volcanic activity, which directly
impacts interstate and international commerce, including
aviation, energy infrastructure, and public safety across
multiple states, particularly in regions like Alaska with
significant volcanic activity. Additionally, Congress has the
authority under Article I, Section 8, Clause 18, which
empowers Congress to make all laws necessary and proper for
carrying out its enumerated powers, including ensuring the
safety and economic stability of the nation through disaster
preparedness and response programs like the National Volcano
Early Warning and Monitoring System.
[Page H1854]

latestAction.text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.

status.title: To amend the John D. Dingell, Jr. Conservation, Management, and Recreation Act to reauthorize the National Volcano Early Warning and Monitoring System.

summaries.text:
This bill reauthorizes through FY2030 the National Volcano Early Warning and Monitoring System of the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS). 

The system, implemented by the USGS in collaboration with the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, includes a monitoring network, volcano observatories, a national data center, and external research grants.
